exemplar.title: âHow effective were McDonaldsâ operations management and supply chain strategies in maintaining global operations when faced with global disruptions to the supply chain from 2020-2025?â

detailedExemplarData.title: âHow effective were McDonaldsâ operations management and supply chain strategies in maintaining global operations when faced with global disruptions to the supply chain from 2020-2025?â

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with a precise, engaging title and comprehensive contextual explanation of global disruptions.
Research question is clearly stated, consistently referenced, and well integrated into the introduction and discussion.
Methodology section outlines an appropriate range of secondary sources and analytical frameworks, demonstrating informed selection.
weaknesses: Research question remains very broad, limiting depth of analysis in some sections.
Rationale section is underdeveloped and ends abruptly, reducing the coherence of the methodological justification.
Justifications for the choice of specific frameworks (SWOT, Porter, ratio analysis) are brief and could be more explicitly linked to the RQ.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation clearly meets the top band descriptor for Focus and Method. The topic is introduced and explained with precision, the research question is explicit and effectively drives the report, and the methodology draws on an appropriate and varied range of sources and analytical tools. Minor issuesâsuch as brevity in the rationale and some repetitionâdo not undermine the overall clarity and coherence. On balance, this warrants the maximum of 6 marks.

strengths: Demonstrates clear and coherent subject knowledge, effectively applying key operations-management concepts to McDonaldâs context.
Source material is mostly relevant and is used to illustrate disruptions and financial ratios.
Terminology and concepts (e.g., supply-chain resilience, profit margin) are applied accurately and consistently.
weaknesses: Several claims rely on generic news sites or uncited LinkedIn posts, leading to uneven credibility.
Some sections are narrative rather than analytical, reducing depth of source integration.
First SWOT table is incomplete, omitting opportunities and threats and weakening the strategic view.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows solid understanding of theory and generally appropriate application of sources, aligning with the upper half of the 3â4 band and approaching 5â6. Although the use of some superficial sources and occasional narrative descriptions limits full excellence, the coherent integration of concepts and mostly accurate terminology justify a strong score of 5 out of 6.

strengths: Arguments are well developed, with logical connections drawn between evidence (quantitative and qualitative) and conclusions.
Report integrates multiple analytical frameworks and financial indicators, aligning them clearly to the research question.
Acknowledgment of limitations in using secondary data shows an emerging critical perspective.
weaknesses: Counterarguments and alternative explanations are rarely considered, limiting the depth of critical evaluation.
Framework applications often remain descriptive before drawing evaluative insights.
Absence of primary research constrains the sophistication of analysis and evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the investigation exhibits good research quality and a well-developed argument, it stops short of the most sophisticated critical evaluation. The student synthesizes a variety of evidence effectively and demonstrates partially critical reflection on data limitations. These strengths place the work at the lower edge of the 7â9 band, meriting 7 out of 12. The score reflects solid critical thinking with room for deeper counter-argument and primary data engagement.

strengths: Report structure is clear and fully meets IB requirements, including title page, table of contents, section headings, analysis, discussion, conclusion, and bibliography.
Formatting of charts and tables is generally consistent, with clear visual presentation.
Layout is clean and largely supports reader navigation.
weaknesses: Page numbers are missing, hindering navigation in the table of contents and throughout the report.
Word count exceeds the 4,000-word limit and is not clearly indicated.
Some headings lack stylistic consistency, and captions for figures need more complete sourcing information.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation satisfies all structural requirements and employs appropriate layout and formatting conventions overall. Although there are omissions in page numbering, word-count declaration, and minor stylistic inconsistencies, these are correctable and sit within the top band for structure and formatting. Therefore, the maximum 4 marks are awarded.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly stated and consistently referenced; however, it remains relatively broad (covering every global disruption between 2020-25 and all of McDonaldâs worldwide operations).  While the discussion connects to it, the breadth limits sharp focus and occasionally encourages descriptive rather than analytical passages.  This fits the descriptor of a clearly stated but only partially focused question, meriting the upper end of the 3-4 band.

The discussion synthesises findings and recognises limitations of secondary data, showing a developed argument.  Evaluation, however, is only partially critical; it seldom weighs alternative explanations or quantifies strategic effectiveness beyond general statements.  This aligns with âwell-developed argument with partially critical evaluationâ (4-6 band, lower end).

The essay demonstrates sound understanding of operations-management conceptsâsupply-chain agility, digitalisation, Triple Bottom Lineâand integrates them coherently with McDonaldâs context.  However, depth is uneven, occasionally sliding into narrative (e.g., recounting pandemic chronology) rather than analytical exposition.  Sources are generally used to substantiate knowledge, so the strand fits the middle of the 3-4 band.

Key BM terminology (supply-chain resilience, profit margin, bargaining power) is employed correctly and consistently.  Minor lapses (e.g., âsunflower oil âvanishesââ rhetoric) and occasional imprecise phrasing prevent full consistency, but overall usage is mostly accurate, warranting the upper half of the 3-4 band.

Analytical sections (SWOT, Five Forces, ratio commentary) draw logical connections between evidence and conclusions, but depth remains moderateâframeworks are presented descriptively before brief evaluative remarks, and counter-arguments are rare.  Support for assertions is partial, matching the lower end of the 4-6 band.

Most cited materials are relevant (annual reports, Macrotrends, World Bank, scholarly articles) and are applied to illustrate disruptions and ratios.  Yet several claims rely on generic news sites or LinkedIn posts and some figures (e.g., sales growth) are asserted without clear citation.  Application therefore fluctuates between precise and superficial, matching âmostly relevant and appropriateâ at the lower end of the 3-4 band.

The methodology section outlines qualitative frameworks (SWOT, Porter, case study) and basic ratio analysis, and a table discusses source types with limitations, showing informed selection across academic, industry and company documents.  Nevertheless, primary research is absent and justification for each chosen tool is brief, so the rangeâwhile appropriateâis not fully convincing as âclear evidence of effective selectionâ.  Thus the work sits solidly in the 3-4 band.

The student consults a range of secondary data and integrates quantitative indicators (margins, sales growth) with qualitative evidence, clearly aligning material to the RQ.  Absence of primary research and reliance on publicly available figures limits excellence, yet the breadth and relevance exceed âadequateâ; hence a mid-band 4-6 score is appropriate.

The student provides headings and embedded charts, but the word count exceeds 4,000 and page numbers are missing in the extract, indicating incomplete adherence to formatting conventions.  Hence some layout considerations are incorrect, fitting the 1-2 band with a score of 2.

The student presents a clear, accurately-phrased topic in the title page and reiterates it in the introduction.  A succinct contextual explanation of global disruptions (pandemic, war, natural disasters) and their relevance to McDonaldâs operations is provided, making the scope and purpose easy to follow.  Minor weaknesses stem from a slightly wordy narrative and some repetition, but overall the topic is communicated effectively and with comprehensive background, aligning best with the 5-6 band.

The essay contains an explicit title page, table of contents, headed sections, analysis, discussion, conclusion and bibliography, fulfilling IB structure requirements.  Internal signposting is clear, though some headings are stylistically inconsistent.  Overall it is âclearly appropriateâ meeting all structural expectations, meriting the 3-4 band (mid-band score).

annotations.comment: Theories are introduced but not integrated into analysis. Explicitly connect Elkingtonâs and Schumpeterâs concepts to McDonaldâs responses.
Analysis of disruptions on page 9 lists impacts descriptively. Deepen critical evaluation by assessing how each disruption severity affected McDonaldâs KPIs.
Figure 1 needs a clearer caption that states data range and source. Revise to âFigure 1: McDonaldâs stock performance during COVID-19 (Jan 2020âDec 2021), source: MarketScreener.â
The title precisely communicates the focus on McDonaldâs operations and supply chain strategies during global disruptions, setting a clear scope for the investigation.

The research question, while clearly stated, covers an exceptionally broad range of disruptions and McDonaldâs operations, limiting analytical focus. Consider narrowing to specific disruption types or regions.
The declared word count of 4,261 exceeds the 4,000-word limit. Edit for conciseness or consider moving descriptive detail to appendices.
The table of contents lists sections without page numbers, hindering navigation. Include accurate page references for each entry.
The âTABLE OF CONTENTSâ header is clear, but user experience suffers without page numbering. Ensure layout conventions are correctly applied.
This introductory description focuses heavily on McDonaldâs network rather than critically identifying inherent supply chain vulnerabilities. Embed analysis of risk factors.
The rationale section is brief and ends mid-sentence. Expand to explicitly connect the researchâs value to the stated question and its real-world implications.
The final sentence of the introduction repeats the research question verbatim. Aim for synthesis by integrating the question into a concise closing thought.
The âMAIN FINDINGSâ section launches into definitions with limited immediate insight. Summarize key findings in bullet form before elaborating.
This paragraph defines disruptions broadly but doesnât yet link back to McDonaldâs strategic actions. Offer context-specific examples early to ground the discussion.
Table 1 accurately presents source types and limitations, but stops short of explaining why each was chosen. Add a brief justification for source selection.
The methodology introduction omits any primary data plan. Consider adding interviews or surveys to enrich the analysis and strengthen findings.
The methodology paragraph outlines frameworks but lacks justification for their selection. Explain how SWOT, Porterâs Five Forces, and ratio analysis align with the RQ.
Framework descriptions on page 7 are comprehensive but remain descriptive. Enhance analysis by directly applying each framework to specific evidence.
Porterâs Five Forces is introduced but not structured by force in the narrative. Break out each force under distinct subheadings for clarity.
Figure 2 is clear but its insights are not discussed. Integrate key observations (e.g., the force with greatest impact) into the surrounding text.
The text following Figure 1 recapâs past content without new insight. Instead, highlight the figureâs most impactful trend in one or two sentences.
Claims about supply chain collaboration on page 11 lack concrete examples. Include specific partnership cases or metrics to substantiate agility.
The digital technologies section asserts advanced analytics use without specific McDonaldâs initiatives. Cite actual systems or pilot projects to validate claims.
Sustainability initiatives are described positively but lack analysis of actual environmental or social impact metrics. Discuss measurable outcomes where possible.
The first SWOT table omits opportunities and threats, preventing a full strategic view. Either complete this matrix here or merge with the subsequent table.
The strengths discussion cites a 4.6% sales increase but citation formatting is inconsistent. Standardize in-text citations per a recognized style guide.
The ratio analysis figure emphasizes gross margin trends but gives less attention to net profit margin. Balance discussion to reflect both metrics.
The discussion section synthesizes findings nicely but omits counterarguments or alternative explanations. Including these would strengthen critical evaluation.
You rightly note reliance on secondary data as a limitation. Strengthen this by proposing specific primary methods (e.g., surveys, interviews) for future research.
Bibliography entries appear inconsistently formatted, with some URLs split across lines. Adopt a single citation style (e.g., APA) and apply it uniformly.
